content: We can frame an agent's intrinsic curiosity drive as a dynamically evolving Linear Temporal Logic Reward Machine, where each unobserved or underexplored memory state corresponds to a reward-generating transition that pushes the agent to satisfy the temporal logic constraint "eventually explore every unvisited information state". This structure replaces ad-hoc curiosity bonuses with verifiable, formal reward shaping that aligns long-term exploratory behavior with the agent's active memory expansion goals.
